Title: The APETALA2–MYBL2 module represses proanthocyanidin biosynthesis by affecting formation of the MBW complex in seeds of Arabidopsis thaliana

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed coat pigmentation and major phenolic secondary metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses proanthocyanidin accumulation in seeds; loss of AP2 increases PA content, while miR172-resistant AP2 overexpression decreases PA content.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AP2 is strongly expressed in the seed coat endothelium where proanthocyanidins accumulate. 2. ap2 mutants show darker seeds and increased PA content, whereas AP2m3 overexpression reduces PA accumulation. 3. AP2 directly activates MYBL2 transcription by binding an AT-rich motif in the MYBL2 promoter. 4. AP2 physically interacts with MYBL2 and promotes formation of AP2–MYBL2–TT8/EGL3 complexes that repress MBW complex activity. 5. AP2 acts genetically upstream of MYBL2, TT2, and TT8 in PA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constantly high throughout seed development; strongly expressed in seed coat endothelium; also detectable in outer seed coat layers and embryo.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in 3–4-day-old developing seeds, especially seed coat endothelium.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of MYBL2 and protein-complex-mediated repression of MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: proanthocyanidin biosynthesis via control of MBW complex formation and late PA gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MYBL2; represses TT2 expression; indirectly represses DFR, ANS, ANR, TT12, TT19, and AHA10 by enhancing MYBL2-mediated inhibition of MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulator of seed PA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR172-mediated translational repression of AP2; AP2m3 is miR172-resistant
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms AP2–MYBL2–TT8 complex and AP2–MYBL2–EGL3 complex via MYBL2; enhances repression of the TT2–TT8–TTG1 MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (ap2-5, ap2-6); miR172-resistant overexpression (AP2m3); DMACA staining and PA quantification; qPCR; in situ hybridization; proAP2::GUS; dual-luciferase assay; promoter mutagenesis; ChIP-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; coIP; genetic epistasis with mybl2, tt2, and tt8
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (overexpression constructs); native AP2 promoter for GUS; proANR and proMYBL2 in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds/siliques; no special external treatment central to PA phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AP2 or miR172-resistant AP2 alleles could be used to reduce seed proanthocyanidin accumulation; use requires caution because AP2 also regulates flower and seed development.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Native AP2 overexpression increased transcript but not protein abundance because AP2 is translationally repressed by miR172; therefore AP2 OE did not alter PA accumulation, whereas miR172-resistant AP2m3 did.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed coat pigmentation and phenolic storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key suppressor of proanthocyanidin biosynthesis; MYBL2 overexpression lowers PA accumulation and is required for AP2-mediated repression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYBL2 is directly activated by AP2 through promoter binding to an AT-rich motif. 2. MYBL2 physically interacts with AP2 and with TT8, GL3, and EGL3. 3. MYBL2 bridges AP2 with TT8/EGL3 to form repressive complexes. 4. MYBL2 suppresses transcriptional activity of the TT2–TT8–TTG1 MBW complex on ANR, and AP2 strengthens this repression. 5. Genetic analysis places MYBL2 downstream of AP2 in PA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced in ap2-6 during seed development; activated by AP2 in developing seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during seed development in the PA-accumulating seed coat context.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression through disruption of MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MBW-complex activation of ANR; inferred to repress other late PA genes such as TT12 and TT19 by disrupting MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key suppressor of the PA pathway and essential downstream mediator of AP2 repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TT2–TT8–TTG1 complex activity; ANR promoter output
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: AP2 directly binds and activates the MYBL2 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with AP2, TT8, GL3, and EGL3; forms AP2–MYBL2–TT8 and AP2–MYBL2–EGL3 complexes; disrupts MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mybl2-1 and mybl2-2 mutants; MYBL2 overexpression; dual-luciferase transactivation assays; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; coIP; truncation interaction mapping; genetic epistasis with AP2m3 overexpression and ap2-6
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (MYBL2 overexpression); proANR and proMYBL2 in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds/siliques; no special external treatment central to PA phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful negative regulatory node for lowering seed PA accumulation, potentially in combination with AP2 manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYBL2 interacts simultaneously with AP2 and TT8/EGL3 at different termini, enabling assembly of repressive ternary complexes.
